scope: custom: combat -- grappling/close-combat substrate (the third plate-counter), Build-13
note: Grappling built bottom-up from the CANONICAL close-combat actions (Disarm/Tie Up/Escape/Retrieve, 3.6 Actions) unified into a grapple phase, + ST1 Strength + the armour matrix (aa) + wa2 hands axis; fills the canon-acknowledged unarmed gap (ED-129). The one new element = the grounded-foe DAGGER-FINISH (point to gaps, bypasses armour mitigation) -- the third historical plate-counter after blunt + point-to-gaps. Self-test 6/6. TOP-DOWN VALIDATED: (A) grappler fells full-harness foe in 4.2 strikes (vs blunt 3.0; cut deflected) -- triad complete; (B) context-gated -- grappler wins 0%% vs a spacing rapier UNARMOURED (reach/tempo beats the closer; closing is risky) but 98%% vs the same fighter IN PLATE (armour slows the spacer + grapple bypasses plate). Historical: Fiore abrazare (foundation under weapon work), ringen, half-swording, the rondel dagger. CAVEAT (self-noted): the 0%% is exaggerated (model lets the spacer always win at range); direction validated, magnitude not. Class A = canonical actions (reused). Class C = the grapple sigma-seeds + the dagger-finish. Reuses aa + wa2 + M1. PROPOSAL -- only the dagger-finish is new; Jordan-vetoable.

entries.quoted_text: Disarm: Off vs (STR+Agi) as Ob. Tie Up: both -2D, blocks Escape. Escape (PP-634): Agi TN7 Ob1, partial = exit + free strike. Retrieve: post-Disarm pickup. Unarmed is outside the weapon matrix (ED-129 gap).
Grapple is a Strength-dominant sigma-contest extending ratified ST1 (bind-win per STR point); grapple is its close-range extreme. Top-down: ringen/abrazare are leverage-and-balance dominant.
A 2H weapon occupies both hands -> poor grapple (wa2 hands); the more-drained fighter grapples worse (asymmetric fatigue); closing surrenders reach/tempo (the cost that makes grappling a choice).
Grapple margin -> outcome: decisive = throw (takedown->prone); marginal = control (Tie Up). Outcomes drawn from the canonical actions.

The grounded/controlled-foe dagger-finish: a point strike on the GAP profile, bypassing armour mitigation (a controlled foe = gap access). The rondel-dagger kill -- the third plate-counter. The one genuinely new mechanic; flagged for ratification.
